title: Anyone can steal all unclaimed queued shares via unrestricted `mintAllocatedShares`
reason: `mintAllocatedShares` calls `mint`, and `mint` enforces `onlyVaultOrQueue`; unauthorized callers revert and state changes (including `allocatedShares` decrement) roll back.

title: Oracle accepts first price report as valid without validation
reason: First report is marked suspicious and not propagated through `handleReport`; finalization requires privileged `ACCEPT_REPORT_ROLE`, so this is primarily trusted-role behavior, not a permissionless exploit.

title: RiskManager.modifySubvaultBalance bypasses limit check for negative changes
reason: Limit is implemented as an upper cap on exposure; negative deltas are intentionally uncapped and external pull failure reverts the transaction, preventing silent underflow-style drain.

title: RiskManager.pendingBalance tracking uses price at modify time instead of settlement
reason: This is a modeling/design choice for pending exposure accounting; no concrete exploitable invariant break or direct protocol-level harm was demonstrated.

title: ShareModule.claimableSharesOf iterates over all assets and queues without gas limits
reason: This is a view/helper path and not a mandatory state-transition path for user funds; main liveness concern from queue iteration in token updates is already captured in F-005.

title: DepositQueue.cancelDepositRequest uses wrong index from oracle checkpoint
FeeManager.calculateFee has no cap on protocol fee accrual period
reason: Linear time-based accrual without a hard cap appears intentional fee economics rather than an implementation flaw.

title: SignatureQueue validates deadline against block.timestamp without tolerance
reason: Strict deadline comparison is expected expiry semantics; lack of grace period is not, by itself, a security vulnerability.
